title: doronicum grandiflorum subsp. carpaticum
intro: Doronicum grandiflorum subsp. carpaticum, also known as the Carpaticum Leopard's Bane, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Carpathian Mountains in Eastern Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Doronicum grandiflorum subsp. carpaticum include its potential to help with digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
